page.content: In the study comparing the Experimental group (n = 9) and Control group (n = 9), the Physical domain showed a pre-score of 42.6 (35.5–56.9) and a post-score of 51.6 (37.7–71.7) with a W statistic of 43.000 and p-value of .015 for the Experimental group, while the Control group had pre- and post-scores of 39.4 (12.4–46.9) and 38.1 (24.2–58.3), respectively, yielding a W of 41.000 and p of .028, with a MW of 38.000 and p of .863. In Strength, the Experimental group reported pre- and post-scores of 50 (30–59.4) and 62.5 (36.3–71.9) with W of 28.000 and p of .017, contrasted with the Control group’s 37.5 (12.5–53.1) and 43.8 (25–62.5), W of 25.000, and p of .063, resulting in a MW of 40.000 and p of .964. For Memory, scores were 62.5 (45.3–82.8) pre and 71.9 (53.1–86.6) post for Experimental (W = 40.000, p = .037), while Control reported 56.3 (32.8–70.3) and 62.5 (46.9–79.7) with W = 36.000 and p = .012, yielding a MW of 30.000 and p of .387. In the Emotion domain, Experimental scores were 75 (55.5–84.7) pre and 83.3 (75–87.4) post (W = 40.500, p = .033), while Control's scores were 58.3 (45.8–73.6) and 66.67 ± 27.78, giving W of 30.000 and p of .092, with a MW of 50.500 and p of .387. Communication scores for Experimental were 75 (60.7–91.1) and 85.7 (62.5–94.6) with W of 21.500 and p of .200, while Control had 67.9 (42.9–80.4) and 67.9 (44.6–83.9) with W of 20.000 and p of .310, resulting in MW of 42.500 and p of .863. Mobility results showed Experimental pre- and post-scores of 67.5 (42.5–74.9) and 75 (51.3–86.3) with W = 36.000 and p = .012, compared to Control's 40 (22.5–53.8) and 52.5 (31.3–58.8), W = 26.000 and p = .043, with a MW of 37.500 and p of .790. Hand Function for Experimental was 15 (0–40) pre and 40 (5–55) post, yielding W of 23.500 and p of .106, while Control had 25 (0–30) and 25 (0–45), W of 18.000 and p of .111, with a MW of 37.000 and p of .752. ADL's scores were 50 (37.5–80.2) and 56.3 (49–86.5) for Experimental (W = 38.000, p = .066), while Control scores were 43.8 (14.6–53.1) and 45.8 (30.2–63.6), W of 38.000 and p of .066, with a MW of 38.000 and p of .863. Social scores for Experimental were 63.9 (29.2–72.3) and 66.7 (53.5–83.3) with W = 34.000 and p = .025, against Control's 36.1 (29.2–51.4) and 50 (41.7–58.3), W of 36.000 and p of .012, yielding a MW of 41.000 and p of 1. Recovery pre- and post-scores for Experimental were 50 (40–55) and 70 (55–80) with W = 28.000 and p = .016, while Control had 40 (40–55) and 60 (45–75), W of 30.500 and p of .078, with a MW of 31.500 and p of .436.
